summary.description: I’m a JavaScript warrior with a solid experience in frontend development (particularly ReactJS) and a strong UX/UI foundation. My love for challenges and eagerness to learn work wonders for me to translate mockups and ideas into proper functional features.

paragraphs: As a triathlete, I’m always testing the latest training methods and techs for the sports I enjoy. I’m in a constant search for ways to improve my technique and performances.

I learnt through endurance sports not to take anything for granted. Fitness is more about consistency than intensity: it takes a long time to build but can vanish in the blink of an eye. I’ve experienced it first hand.
I believe that being able to question oneself, seek potential solutions, try them out, fail and repeat is key to eventually succeed.
And I’m willing to do the work.
Working with NGOs to protect animals and the environment is an essential part of my life.
Besides fulfilling my desire to have a positive impact and educate the public on the issues we face as a society, it’s an excellent way for me to improve my communication skills and challenge myself.

I’m an introvert and engaging conversations with strangers felt like an impossible task to do at first. But, after throwing myself under the bus -and a few awkward moments-, I managed to be more effective with my speech and arguments.
My new found ability to respond to the person in front of me is enhanced by my more innate listening and empathizing skills. It really is a superpower.
Living with a companion animal is a gift as well as a challenge.
Prior to having my dog Satay in my life, I dreamt of an active lifestyle, running regularly with my dog. But this just isn’t who he is. He’s more of a couch potato.
If you hear snoring during our One-on-One, that’s him!
I like to believe that, even though he’s not the dog I dreamt of, he rather is the one I needed in my life. He taught me an important skill I very much lacked: patience.

skills.sass: I tend to prefer CSS over Javascript to handle styles everywhere I can, including for animations and transitions. It’s faster, smoother and more performant but it lacks a lot of features that are fortunately provided by SASS. From variables to mixins and everything in between, it makes my styles more scalable and easier to read and update.
skills.javascript: This is my favorite language. I love how I’m able to do everything I set my mind to quite easily and its syntax and semantics makes my code very expressive and declarative. I’ve been working with JS and JS frameworks for years now and I keep on learning new features all the time! I really enjoy diving deep into it.
skills.react: This is my favorite framework and the one I’m more experienced in working with. I love its functional components-based approach, the usage of hooks - both built-in and homemade - and its virtual DOM that’s definitely a game changer! This website has been created with it.
skills.vue: Even though I’m less experienced in working with Vue, learning React helped me greatly in learning Vue afterwards. They're similar in many ways, which eases the transition from one to another, but I find Vue and its Composition API very promising and interesting.
skills.typescript: Types are the one thing really lacking in Javascript and I’m glad there’s Typescript to make up for it. It revolutionized the way I code and debug my code. I love how it makes it clear what a component/function is looking for (as a prop or argument for example) and how easier it is to find where some mistakes come from. It has become essential to me.
skills.django: I've used Python before but Django is quite new to me. I've learnt a lot about it while building this website.
skills.cypress: Unit testing is essential and very powerful but, to test an app/worflow as a whole, end-to-end testing is required and Cypress is a great tool for it. I was introduced to it while working for AB Tasty and loved it! It allows to easily track and monitor the test coverage of the UI, prevent regressions and ensure that critical flows are tested.
skills.figma: I’ve previously been working with Photoshop, Xd or Sketch for mockups and different projects but Figma definitely stands out, at least for web design. It’s visibly made for both designers and developers and I really appreciate it. This website mockup has been created using it and its link is available on the footer.
skills.github: This website's code is stores on GitHub and the link to it is available on the footer.
skills.gitlab: I first was introduced to GitLab and its CI pipeline while working for AB Tasty. It’s an efficient and intuitive tool for continuous integration and delivery that I really enjoy working with.
skills.postgresql: I recently was introduced to PostgreSQL but was already familiar with SQL requests prior. Its robust set of features is perfect for complex queries and makes it enjoyable to work with.
skills.jira: It’s the perfect tool for bug tracking and agile project management. I’ve worked with it on many projects with very different organization styles and the least we can say is that it delivers!

description: Software development & programming
Adaptation & innovation in digital technologies
Design & management of information systems
Management & communication for teams and projects

Creation of components and features related to events (sport events, elections, …)
Creation of emailing templates
Implementation of components for events or promotional purposes
Feasibility analysis and time estimation of campaigns based on a brief provided by the client
Application of modifications on top of the client's existing website (usage of MutationObserver for SPA websites)
Creation of a command line (NodeJS) which uses AB Tasty's API to allow the TSE team to save time on repetitive tasks
Technical training for the CSM team
Creation of components/features for the B2B dashboard
Participation in the design and development of components for the design system
Unit & e2e testing (implementation of Cypress)
Continuous deployment and work on pipeline's jobs (Gitlab CI)
Focus on performance, maintainability, reusability, browser compatibility and GDPR compliance
Code review
Investigation and bug fix
Implementation of components for events or promotional purposes
Feasibility analysis and time estimation of campaigns based on a brief provided by the client
Application of modifications on top of the client's existing website (usage of MutationObserver for SPA websites)
Focus on accessibility and keyboard navigation
